rubric_text:
In file mailbox.json (in `external_data/final`), There must be a sent email to david.park@pathfinderbilling.com with exact subject "Pattern Recurrence Alert: Dr. Sarah Chen — CO-16 — 5 Instances" and exact body (no partial match accepted): "Pattern Recurrence Report — 04/24/2026

This is Maria Santos, Resolution Auditor, reporting a pattern recurrence for Dr. Sarah Chen, NPI: 1234567890.

Denial Code: CO-16 — RARC: N382 — 5 instances identified.

Affected Claims:

1. Claim ID: CLM-2026-0234 — Patient ID: PAT-1008 — DOS: 01/15/2026 — CPT: 99214 — Status: Resubmitted

2. Claim ID: CLM-2026-0356 — Patient ID: PAT-1012 — DOS: 01/28/2026 — CPT: 99213 — Status: Resubmitted

3. Claim ID: CLM-2026-0489 — Patient ID: PAT-1019 — DOS: 02/10/2026 — CPT: 99214 — Status: Resubmitted

4. Claim ID: CLM-2026-0612 — Patient ID: PAT-1027 — DOS: 02/22/2026 — CPT: 99213 — Status: Resubmitted

5. Claim ID: CLM-2026-0892 — Patient ID: PAT-1042 — DOS: 03/15/2026 — CPT: 99214 — Status: Resubmitted

Assessment: This pattern suggests a systemic submission error. Requesting management review to determine whether a corrective action plan or direct clinic intervention is warranted.

Escalation Log Entry ID: ESC-2026-0008. Escalation Category: Operational. Escalation Reason: Pattern Recurrence." where "ESC-2026-0008" could be "ESC-2026-0009"
In `mailbox.json`, there must be exactly three sent emails in total.
In `PBC_Claim_Register.xlsx`, there must be a table with exactly 40 total rows (including the header row). No leniency, must be exactly 40.

In `PBC_Claim_Register.xlsx`, there must be one row in the table with the following exact values: "CLM-2026-0845", "PAT-1021", "Jennifer Walsh", "03/10/2026", "Dr. Sarah Chen", "1234567890", "1122334455", "BlueCross TX", "BCTX1", "99213", "J06.9", BLANK, "1", BLANK, "Paid - Full", "$180.00", "$142.00", "$38.00", "$0.00", "Reconciled", BLANK, BLANK, BLANK.

In `PBC_Claim_Register.xlsx`, there must be one row in the table with the exact values: "CLM-2026-0878", "PAT-1035", "David Kim", "03/12/2026", "Dr. James Miller", "2345678901", BLANK, "BlueCross TX", "BCTX1", "99214", "M54.5", BLANK, "1", BLANK, "Paid - Full", "$285.00", "$228.00", "$57.00", "$0.00", "Reconciled", BLANK, BLANK, BLANK.

In `PBC_Claim_Register.xlsx`, there must be one row in the table with the following exact values: "CLM-2026-0892", "PAT-1042", "Michael Torres", "03/15/2026", "Dr. Sarah Chen", "1234567890", "1122334455", "BlueCross TX", "BCTX1", "99214", "E11.65", BLANK, "1", BLANK, "Resubmitted" ["Submitted" is also acceptable], "$285.00", "$0.00", "$285.00", "$0.00", BLANK, BLANK, "CO-16", "N382".

In `PBC_Claim_Register.xlsx`, there must be one row in the table with the following exact values: "CLM-2026-1104", "PAT-1058", "Linda Park", "03/20/2026", "Dr. James Miller", "2345678901", BLANK, "BlueCross TX", "BCTX1", "29881", "M23.211", BLANK, "1", BLANK, "Paid - Full", "$2,200.00", "$1,850.00", "$350.00", "$0.00", "Reconciled", BLANK, BLANK, BLANK.

In `PBC_Claim_Register.xlsx`, there must be one row in the table with the following exact values [No partial matches acceptable, must match every value]: "CLM-2026-1105", "PAT-1058", "Linda Park", "03/20/2026", "Dr. James Miller", "2345678901", BLANK, "BlueCross TX", "BCTX1", "29876", "M65.862", BLANK, "1", BLANK, "Resubmitted" ["Submitted" is also acceptable], "$1,850.00", "$0.00", "$1,850.00", "$0.00", BLANK, BLANK, "CO-97", "N520".

In `PBC_Claim_Register.xlsx`, there must be one row in the table with the exact values [No partial matches acceptable, must match every value]: "CLM-2026-0967", "PAT-1063", "Robert Daniels", "03/08/2026", "Dr. Amanda Foster", "3456789012", BLANK, "BlueCross TX", "BCTX1", "27447", "M17.11", BLANK, "1", BLANK, "In Recovery", "$7,500.00", "$4,200.00", "$3,300.00", "$0.00", "Held - Contractual Variance", BLANK, "CO-45", "N130".

In `PBC_Claim_Register.xlsx`, there must be one row in the table with the exact values [No partial matches acceptable]: "CLM-2026-1106" (or "CLM-2026-1107"), "PAT-1042", "Michael Torres", "03/15/2026", "Dr. Sarah Chen", "1234567890", "1122334455", "BlueCross TX", "BCTX1", "99214", "E11.65", BLANK, "7", "CLM-2026-0892", "Submitted", "$285.00", "$0.00", "$0.00", "$0.00", BLANK, BLANK, BLANK, BLANK.

In `PBC_Claim_Register.xlsx`, there must be one row in the table with the exact values: "CLM-2026-1107" (or "CLM-2026-1106"), "PAT-1058", "Linda Park", "03/20/2026", "Dr. James Miller", "2345678901", BLANK, "BlueCross TX", "BCTX1", "29876", "M65.862", "LT", "7", "CLM-2026-1105", "Submitted", "$1,850.00", "$0.00", "$0.00", "$0.00", BLANK, BLANK, BLANK, BLANK.
In `PBC_Claim_Register.xlsx`, there must be no duplicate rows or `Claim_ID`s.
In `PBC_Denial_Worklist.xlsx`, there must be a table with 11 total rows (including the header row).

In `PBC_Denial_Worklist.xlsx`, Row 9, 10, or 11 must contain the exact values: "DEN-2026-0028" (where 28 can be 29 or 30), "CLM-2026-0892", "PAT-1042", "Michael Torres", "03/15/2026", "BlueCross TX", "CO-16", "N382", "Technical/Administrative", "Correct and Resubmit", "Resubmitted", "Maria Santos", "04/24/2026", and BLANK (empty cell) in consecutive columns.

In `PBC_Denial_Worklist.xlsx`, Row 9, 10, or 11 must contain the exact values: "DEN-2026-0029" (where 29 can be 28 or 30), "CLM-2026-1105", "PAT-1058", "Linda Park", "03/20/2026", "BlueCross TX", "CO-97", "N520", "Technical/Administrative", "Add Modifier and Resubmit", "Resubmitted", "Maria Santos", "04/24/2026", and the last cell must be BLANK.

In `PBC_Denial_Worklist.xlsx`, Row 9, 10, or 11 must contain the following exact values: "DEN-2026-0030" (where 30 can be 29 or 38), "CLM-2026-0967", "PAT-1063", "Robert Daniels", "03/08/2026", "BlueCross TX", "CO-45", "N130", "Contractual/Payer Error", "Appeal - Contractual Rate Dispute", "Escalated" ["Appealed" also acceptable], "Maria Santos", "04/24/2026", and the last cell must be BLANK.
verifier_code:
from pathlib import Path
import openpyxl
import re
import datetime

def verify(workspace_path, external_services_path=None):
    # Try to find the file with glob for flexibility
    filepath = None
    candidates = list(Path(workspace_path).glob("**/PBC_Denial_Worklist.xlsx"))
    if candidates:
        filepath = candidates[0]
    else:
        fp = Path(workspace_path) / "PBC_Denial_Worklist.xlsx"
        if fp.exists():
            filepath = fp

    if filepath is None or not filepath.exists():
        return {"pass": False, "score": 0.0, "feedback": f"File not found: PBC_Denial_Worklist.xlsx in {workspace_path}"}

    try:
        wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(filepath)
        ws = wb.active
    except Exception as e:
        return {"pass": False, "score": 0.0, "feedback": f"Could not open workbook: {e}"}

    # The DEN ID can be DEN-2026-0029, DEN-2026-0030, DEN-2026-0038, DEN-2026-0039
    valid_den_ids = ["den-2026-0029", "den-2026-0030", "den-2026-0038", "den-2026-0039"]

    # Expected values for columns after DEN ID (indices 1..12)
    # Index 0: DEN ID (special)
    # Index 1: CLM-2026-0967
    # Index 2: PAT-1063
    # Index 3: Robert Daniels
    # Index 4: 03/08/2026
    # Index 5: BlueCross TX
    # Index 6: CO-45
    # Index 7: N130
    # Index 8: Contractual/Payer Error
    # Index 9: Appeal - Contractual Rate Dispute
    # Index 10: Escalated or Appealed
    # Index 11: Maria Santos
    # Index 12: 04/24/2026
    # Index 13: BLANK

    expected_values = [
        None,  # placeholder for DEN ID
        "CLM-2026-0967",
        "PAT-1063",
        "Robert Daniels",
        "03/08/2026",
        "BlueCross TX",
        "CO-45",
        "N130",
        "Contractual/Payer Error",
        "Appeal - Contractual Rate Dispute",
        "Escalated",  # also accept "Appealed"
        "Maria Santos",
        "04/24/2026"
    ]

    def normalize(val):
        if val is None:
            return ""
        s = str(val).strip()
        return s

    def norm_date(s):
        """Normalize date string to MM/DD/YYYY format."""
        date_patterns = [
            (r'(\d{1,2})/(\d{1,2})/(\d{4})', lambda m: f"{int(m.group(1)):02d}/{int(m.group(2)):02d}/{m.group(3)}"),
            (r'(\d{4})-(\d{1,2})-(\d{1,2})', lambda m: f"{int(m.group(2)):02d}/{int(m.group(3)):02d}/{m.group(1)}")
        ]
        for pat, fmt in date_patterns:
            m = re.match(pat, s.strip())
            if m:
                return fmt(m)
        return s.strip()

    def cell_to_normalized_string(actual):
        """Convert a cell value to a normalized comparable string."""
        if actual is None:
            return ""
        if isinstance(actual, (datetime.datetime, datetime.date)):
            d = actual if isinstance(actual, datetime.date) else actual.date()
            return f"{d.month:02d}/{d.day:02d}/{d.year}"
        return str(actual).strip()

    def values_match(actual, expected, col_index):
        a = cell_to_normalized_string(actual).lower()
        e = expected.lower().strip()
        # Special handling for column 10 (Escalated / Appealed)
        if col_index == 10:
            return a in ["escalated", "appealed"]
        if a == e:
            return True
        # Try date normalization
        if norm_date(a) == norm_date(e):
            return True
        # Loose matching: strip punctuation differences
        a_clean = re.sub(r'[\s\-/]+', '', a)
        e_clean = re.sub(r'[\s\-/]+', '', e)
        if a_clean == e_clean:
            return True
        return False

    def is_blank(val):
        return val is None or str(val).strip() == ""

    rows_to_check = list(range(2, ws.max_row + 1))  # Check all data rows, but prioritize 9-11
    priority_rows = [9, 10, 11]
    other_rows = [r for r in rows_to_check if r not in priority_rows]
    ordered_rows = priority_rows + other_rows

    best_score = 0
    best_details = []
    best_row = None

    for row_num in ordered_rows:
        if row_num > ws.max_row:
            continue
        row_cells = []
        for col in range(1, ws.max_column + 1):
            row_cells.append(ws.cell(row=row_num, column=col).value)

        if len(row_cells) < len(expected_values):
            continue

        all_match = True
        match_details = []
        matched_count = 0

        # Check column 1 (DEN ID)
        col0_val = cell_to_normalized_string(row_cells[0]).lower()
        if col0_val in valid_den_ids:
            match_details.append(f"Col 1: '{normalize(row_cells[0])}' matches DEN ID pattern")
            matched_count += 1
        else:
            all_match = False
            match_details.append(f"Col 1: '{normalize(row_cells[0])}' not in valid DEN IDs {valid_den_ids}")

        # Check remaining expected values (index 1 onwards)
        for i in range(1, len(expected_values)):
            exp = expected_values[i]
            if i < len(row_cells):
                if values_match(row_cells[i], exp, i):
                    match_details.append(f"Col {i+1}: '{normalize(row_cells[i])}' matches '{exp}'")
                    matched_count += 1
                else:
                    all_match = False
                    match_details.append(f"Col {i+1}: '{cell_to_normalized_string(row_cells[i])}' != '{exp}'")
            else:
                all_match = False
                match_details.append(f"Col {i+1}: missing")

        # Check last cell (column index len(expected_values)) is blank
        last_col_idx = len(expected_values)
        if last_col_idx < len(row_cells):
            if not is_blank(row_cells[last_col_idx]):
                all_match = False
                match_details.append(f"Col {last_col_idx+1} (last): expected BLANK, got '{normalize(row_cells[last_col_idx])}'")
            else:
                match_details.append(f"Col {last_col_idx+1} (last): BLANK as expected")
                matched_count += 1
        else:
            match_details.append(f"Col {last_col_idx+1} (last): BLANK (beyond data range)")
            matched_count += 1

        total_checks = len(expected_values) + 1  # expected values + blank check
        row_score = matched_count / total_checks

        if all_match:
            return {"pass": True, "score": 1.0, "feedback": f"Row {row_num} matches all expected values. Details: {'; '.join(match_details)}"}

        if row_score > best_score:
            best_score = row_score
            best_details = match_details
            best_row = row_num

    # If none matched fully, provide diagnostics for rows 9-11
    diagnostics = []
    for row_num in priority_rows:
        if row_num > ws.max_row:
            diagnostics.append(f"Row {row_num}: (beyond max_row {ws.max_row})")
            continue
        row_cells = []
        for col in range(1, ws.max_column + 1):
            row_cells.append(ws.cell(row=row_num, column=col).value)
        diagnostics.append(f"Row {row_num}: {[cell_to_normalized_string(c) for c in row_cells]}")

    feedback = (f"No matching row found. Valid DEN IDs: {valid_den_ids}. "
                f"Expected remaining values: {expected_values[1:]} + BLANK (col 10 also accepts 'Appealed'). "
                f"Best partial score: {best_score:.2f} in row {best_row}. "
                f"Best match details: {'; '.join(best_details)}. "
                f"Rows 9-11: {'; '.join(diagnostics)}")
    return {"pass": False, "score": best_score * 0.5, "feedback": feedback}

rubric_text: In `PBC_Denial_Worklist.xlsx`, there must be no duplicate rows or Denial IDs.
In `PBC_Escalation_Log.xlsx`, there must be a table with 5 total rows (including the header row).

In `PBC_Escalation_Log.xlsx`, Row 4 or 5 must contain the following exact values: "ESC-2026-0008" (or "ESC-2026-0009"), "04/24/2026", "CLM-2026-0234, CLM-2026-0356, CLM-2026-0489, CLM-2026-0612, CLM-2026-0892" (in any order), "Operational", "Pattern Recurrence", "Maria Santos", "David Park", "Open", and a BLANK cell.

In `PBC_Escalation_Log.xlsx`, Row 4 or 5 must contain the exact values: "ESC-2026-0009" (or "ESC-2026-0008"), "04/24/2026", "CLM-2026-0967", "Financial", "Payer Dispute", "Maria Santos", "David Park", "Open", and BLANK (empty cell).
In `PBC_Escalation_Log.xlsx`, there must be no duplicate rows or Escalation IDs
In `PBC_RA_Queue.xlsx`, there must be a table with 11 total rows (including the header row).

In `PBC_RA_Queue.xlsx`, Row 9, 10, or 11 must contain the exact values: "RA-2026-0031" (where 31 can be 32 or 33), "04/24/2026", "CLM-2026-0892", "PAT-1042", "ERA Processing", [Any priority value is acceptable], "Maria Santos", "Resolved", "04/24/2026". (where the last two cells could be "Open", BLANK instead)

In `PBC_RA_Queue.xlsx`, row 9, 10, or 11 must contain the exact values: "RA-2026-0032" (where 32 can be 31 or 33), "04/24/2026", "CLM-2026-1105", "PAT-1058", "ERA Processing", [Any priority value is acceptable], "Maria Santos", "Resolved", "04/24/2026". (where the last two cells could be "Open", BLANK instead)

In `PBC_RA_Queue.xlsx`, Row 9, 10, or 11 must contain the exact values: "RA-2026-0033" (where 33 can be 32 or 31), "04/24/2026", "CLM-2026-0967", "PAT-1063", "ERA Processing", [Any priority value is acceptable], "Maria Santos", "Open", and a BLANK cell (in the appropriate columns).
In `PBC_RA_Queue.xlsx`, there must be no duplicate rows or Queue Entry IDs.
In `slack_data.json` (in `external_data/final`), there must be exactly 3 total messages in #general and exactly 8 total messages in #rcm-team.
